name: Steen, M. W.
description: At college M. W. has been a very steady, conscientious student, always willing to do more than his share of volunteer work. Never has he been found without a willing spirit of helpfulness to his fellowmen. There is no question as to his success in the field, for he is an all-round, clean-cut S. T. C. man.
text: At college M. W. has been a very steady, conscientious student, always willing to do more than his share of volunteer work. Never has he been found without a willing spirit of helpfulness to his fellowmen. There is no question as to his success in the field, for he is an all-round, clean-cut S. T. C. man.

name: Woods, D. Johnson
motto: "One thing is forever good, and that one thing is success."
description: Like the other Johnsons from Scott County who preceded him at S. T. C., has emerged with a spotless reputation from the temptations of college life. He has an alert mind, high spirits, and excellent poise.
text:
"One thing is forever good, and that one thing is success."
Like the other Johnsons from Scott County who preceded him at S. T. C., has emerged with a spotless reputation from the temptations of college life. He has an alert mind, high spirits, and excellent poise.

name: Dale, J. E.
motto:
"I have no wife to worry my life,
So I paddle my own canoe."
description: You have heard of "types." Well, is as stalwart, well-built, red-blooded a type of Mississippi manhood as you are likely to find. He is conceded to be "the best" on a football line and is not by any means "the worst" in the classroom.
text:
"I have no wife to worry my life,
So I paddle my own canoe."
You have heard of "types." Well, is as stalwart, well-built, red-blooded a type of Mississippi manhood as you are likely to find. He is conceded to be "the best" on a football line and is not by any means "the worst" in the classroom.

name: Segrest, Annie Lee
motto:
"The longer you know her the sweeter she grows,
She is one of those people that is always composed."
description: Though you search the world over, you will not find a more capable or finer girl than Annie Lee. She has won the love, confidence, and admiration of all her fellow students.
text:
"The longer you know her the sweeter she grows,
She is one of those people that is always composed."
Though you search the world over, you will not find a more capable or finer girl than Annie Lee. She has won the love, confidence, and admiration of all her fellow students.

name: Hadden, Mrs. S. T.
motto:
"A wish of friendship
Blended with a heart of love;
An extract of goodness
From the heavens above."
description: Mrs. Hadden is a true friend, an earnest worker, and a loyal companion. She has but one hobby, "Sam."
text:
"A wish of friendship
Blended with a heart of love;
An extract of goodness
From the heavens above."
Mrs. Hadden is a true friend, an earnest worker, and a loyal companion. She has but one hobby, "Sam."

name: Miller, R. S.
description: Although one of the youngest members of the Class of '27, Ray is by no means the least in achievements. As an earnest student, a hard worker, a partaker in every activity of the college, a loyal friend and companion, Ray is unexcelled. In matters of business he is as serious as a veteran, while in play he lays aside every care and enters into the spirit of it in a whole-hearted and "happy-go-lucky" way. He says "love 'em and leave 'em" is his theory, but the people of Foxworth know he does not always practice it.
text: Although one of the youngest members of the Class of '27, Ray is by no means the least in achievements. As an earnest student, a hard worker, a partaker in every activity of the college, a loyal friend and companion, Ray is unexcelled. In matters of business he is as serious as a veteran, while in play he lays aside every care and enters into the spirit of it in a whole-hearted and "happy-go-lucky" way. He says "love 'em and leave 'em" is his theory, but the people of Foxworth know he does not always practice it.

name: Lewis, Beulah Mildred
motto:
"Oh, conqueror of cities, what art thou
When compared to this conqueror of hearts?"
clubs: Y. W. C. A. Membership Committee, '24, '25
description: While not advertised as such, Mildred is one of the attractions of the S. T. C. campus. She is much too clever to worry over studies, and, in fact, is possessed of a disposition that does not permit unrest of mind.
text:
"Oh, conqueror of cities, what art thou
When compared to this conqueror of hearts?"
While not advertised as such, Mildred is one of the attractions of the S. T. C. campus. She is much too clever to worry over studies, and, in fact, is possessed of a disposition that does not permit unrest of mind.

name: Hinton, L. T.
motto: "I've taken my fun where I've found it, And now I must pay for my fun!"
description: "PeeWee," the butt of a thousand jokes and the author of as many more, is a likable chap, who is equally at home in the classroom or ballroom. His perpetual optimism, good cheer, and ability to make a saxophone "cry" have won for him a lasting place in the memory of his classmates.
text:
"I've taken my fun where I've found it, And now I must pay for my fun!"
"PeeWee," the butt of a thousand jokes and the author of as many more, is a likable chap, who is equally at home in the classroom or ballroom. His perpetual optimism, good cheer, and ability to make a saxophone "cry" have won for him a lasting place in the memory of his classmates.

name: Hogan, Sibyl
description: "She is pleasant with all, She is jolly with each, The school will be fortunate That gets her to teach."
text: "She is pleasant with all, She is jolly with each, The school will be fortunate That gets her to teach."

name: D'Olive, Annie Louise
description: Annie Louise is an alluring brunette with dark dreamy eyes and a charming smile. She is an excellent student with a fine, noble character.
text: Annie Louise is an alluring brunette with dark dreamy eyes and a charming smile. She is an excellent student with a fine, noble character.

name: Davis, Alma
motto: "Sincere in thought, honest in action, With a heart ever kind and true."
description: In Alma are combined those qualities of duty, strength, and kindness which have won for her a lasting place in the hearts of her fellow students. The ideals for which she strives are always the best. All who know her wish for her a happy and prosperous future.
text:
"Sincere in thought, honest in action, With a heart ever kind and true."
In Alma are combined those qualities of duty, strength, and kindness which have won for her a lasting place in the hearts of her fellow students. The ideals for which she strives are always the best. All who know her wish for her a happy and prosperous future.

name: Lewis, S. Truman
clubs: Chairman Program Committee Platonian Literary Society, '25, '26
Program Committee Y. M. C. A., '24, '25
Representative of the College at Students' Conference, '24
Representative of the College at Y. M. C. A. and Y. W. C. A. Conference, '25
description: There must be something in a name, for Truman (True man) lives up to his. Many fine things might be said of him, of his courage, his sincerity, his ability, and his honor; but, if one were to try to tell in one word why everybody loves and trusts him, that one word would be nobility.
text: There must be something in a name, for Truman (True man) lives up to his. Many fine things might be said of him, of his courage, his sincerity, his ability, and his honor; but, if one were to try to tell in one word why everybody loves and trusts him, that one word would be nobility.

name: Tidwell, Ava
description: A girl who can make a success of her college life, who can win friends with one smile, who can even sew and cook as well as a home economics major—who could this be but Ava? Always smiling, always gay, That's Ava, every day.
text: A girl who can make a success of her college life, who can win friends with one smile, who can even sew and cook as well as a home economics major—who could this be but Ava? Always smiling, always gay, That's Ava, every day.

name: Grimmett, Dorothy Earle
description: We are grateful to Louisiana for sending us this lovable girl. A rare combination of wit, sense, stability, optimism, and loyalty is "Dot".
text: We are grateful to Louisiana for sending us this lovable girl. A rare combination of wit, sense, stability, optimism, and loyalty is "Dot".

name: Waites, Ottis
motto: "If it be a gentleman and a friend ye seek, ye have found him."
description: "Al" is a handsome, courteous, friendly chap, who combines work and pleasure so skillfully that neither is ever slighted. That explains his popularity with both faculty and students, as well as the ease with which he charms one of our most attractive co-eds. As he was to his comrades on the gridiron, so he is to his schoolmates, always the "Same Old Al".
text:
"If it be a gentleman and a friend ye seek, ye have found him."
"Al" is a handsome, courteous, friendly chap, who combines work and pleasure so skillfully that neither is ever slighted. That explains his popularity with both faculty and students, as well as the ease with which he charms one of our most attractive co-eds. As he was to his comrades on the gridiron, so he is to his schoolmates, always the "Same Old Al".

name: Richey, A. T.
description: For many years Richey served as one of Mississippi's faithful teachers, struggling under adverse conditions. The establishment of the Mississippi Normal College in 1912 gave him a vision of greater usefulness and a conviction that "the old order changeth, yielding place to new." He has never failed to make the honor roll, and for the first quarter of '27, his name was on the "All A" list. With his B.S. in sight he is now dreaming of an M.A. from "Ole Miss" or Peabody.
text: For many years Richey served as one of Mississippi's faithful teachers, struggling under adverse conditions. The establishment of the Mississippi Normal College in 1912 gave him a vision of greater usefulness and a conviction that "the old order changeth, yielding place to new." He has never failed to make the honor roll, and for the first quarter of '27, his name was on the "All A" list. With his B.S. in sight he is now dreaming of an M.A. from "Ole Miss" or Peabody.

name: Wilkinson, Inez
motto: "The best of sports, the truest of friends, 'Red' is the kind that always wins."
description: What's the matter with "Red"? She's all right. Who says so? We all say so! By the way, doesn't she play ball? Well, I'll say so!
text:
"The best of sports, the truest of friends, 'Red' is the kind that always wins."
What's the matter with "Red"? She's all right. Who says so? We all say so! By the way, doesn't she play ball? Well, I'll say so!

name: Ellis, Alice Rebecca
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer World Affairs Club, '27
description: Ready for work, ready for fun, ready for life—that's Alice. She shows how fine and dependable human nature may be. There are girls and girls, but only one "Alice."
text: Ready for work, ready for fun, ready for life—that's Alice. She shows how fine and dependable human nature may be. There are girls and girls, but only one "Alice."

name: Vinson, Bess
motto: "Give to the world the best that you have, And the best will come back to you."
description: Bess is a quiet young lady who has acquired both love and friendship at S. T. C. Her policy of service insures her a place in the lives of many; her charming manner and adorable disposition assure her a place in the hearts of all.
text:
"Give to the world the best that you have, And the best will come back to you."
Bess is a quiet young lady who has acquired both love and friendship at S. T. C. Her policy of service insures her a place in the lives of many; her charming manner and adorable disposition assure her a place in the hearts of all.

name: Beemon, F. M.
motto: "A perfect woman nobly planned, To love, to comfort and command."
description: Household duties have not kept Mrs. Beemon from being an excellent student. She is always at her post of duty with a smile. In her college work she majored in Home Economics, but she says that she has a real major and minor at home.
text:
"A perfect woman nobly planned, To love, to comfort and command."
Household duties have not kept Mrs. Beemon from being an excellent student. She is always at her post of duty with a smile. In her college work she majored in Home Economics, but she says that she has a real major and minor at home.

name: Selby, Elizabeth
motto: "And still they gazed, and still the wonder grew, That one small head could carry all she knew."
description: "All A" List Five Quarters.
text:
"And still they gazed, and still the wonder grew, That one small head could carry all she knew."
"All A" List Five Quarters.

name: Sharborough, Hester
motto:
"She would talk to a stone
Or to the grass blade by it;
I wonder where she's gone
If the Lord loves quiet."
description: We are still wondering about Hester. We do not know what she is going to do, or when she is going to do it, or whether she is going to do it at all. Neither does she.
text:
"She would talk to a stone
Or to the grass blade by it;
I wonder where she's gone
If the Lord loves quiet."
We are still wondering about Hester. We do not know what she is going to do, or when she is going to do it, or whether she is going to do it at all. Neither does she.

name: Ellington, Maggie Mae
motto:
"Studious, true, lovable, and kind,
A more earnest friend you'll never find."
text:
"Studious, true, lovable, and kind,
A more earnest friend you'll never find."

name: Callender, Mamie
motto:
"Mamie up-to-date—she surely must be,
For her name is Callender, it seems to me;
Through every day she is sincere, kind and true,
Always ready in her calm, quiet way,
To lighten a burden as she goes on her way."
clubs: Membership Committee Y. W. C. A., '27
text:
"Mamie up-to-date—she surely must be,
For her name is Callender, it seems to me;
Through every day she is sincere, kind and true,
Always ready in her calm, quiet way,
To lighten a burden as she goes on her way."

name: Nutt, Oneita
motto: "She lives in all hearts, for all of us know, The earth has never borne a nobler."
description: To our Oneita Abraham dedicated his faith, Job his patience, and Athene her spirit of gentleness and purity.
text:
"She lives in all hearts, for all of us know, The earth has never borne a nobler."
To our Oneita Abraham dedicated his faith, Job his patience, and Athene her spirit of gentleness and purity.

name: Herrington, Sidney Orbrey
description: He slipped in as a summer student in 1920. With the '27 session for a finishing touch, he believes that summer theory and winter practice is a good combination. Since he is one of the few whose grades are above the "80" mark, he has carried "Jasper's" spirit of holding high the banner until victory is won.
text: He slipped in as a summer student in 1920. With the '27 session for a finishing touch, he believes that summer theory and winter practice is a good combination. Since he is one of the few whose grades are above the "80" mark, he has carried "Jasper's" spirit of holding high the banner until victory is won.

name: Phillips, Gertrude
clubs: Chairman Music Committee Mississippian Literary Society, '27
description: With her charming personality she wins friends and keeps them. She is faithful, kind, and persevering, one to whom "Daddy Joe" pays tribute—a royal, second-mile girl.
text: With her charming personality she wins friends and keeps them. She is faithful, kind, and persevering, one to whom "Daddy Joe" pays tribute—a royal, second-mile girl.

name: Daughdrill, Lyda
motto:
"To any class, of any kind,
She'd be an acquisition;
She's amiable to everyone—
It's just her disposition."
text:
"To any class, of any kind,
She'd be an acquisition;
She's amiable to everyone—
It's just her disposition."

name: Ellis, Margaret
motto:
"She's extremely conscientious,
And when there's work to do
If Margaret has a hand in it
She's sure to put it through."
text:
"She's extremely conscientious,
And when there's work to do
If Margaret has a hand in it
She's sure to put it through."

name: Gardner, Gertrude A.
description: Gertrude is an eager, progressive student with high ideals, gentle manners, and a readiness to co-operate in everything that is good.
text: Gertrude is an eager, progressive student with high ideals, gentle manners, and a readiness to co-operate in everything that is good.

name: Waldrop, Gladys
motto: "Far may we seek before we find A girl so gentle and so kind."
description: The friendly and unselfish manner of Gladys has won her many friends.
text:
"Far may we seek before we find A girl so gentle and so kind."
The friendly and unselfish manner of Gladys has won her many friends.

name: Nix, A. L.
motto: "He holds no parley with unmanly fears; Where duty bids, he confidently steers."
description: His nerve and determination on the athletic field, and his scholastic ability have made him a leader at S. T. C.
text:
"He holds no parley with unmanly fears; Where duty bids, he confidently steers."
His nerve and determination on the athletic field, and his scholastic ability have made him a leader at S. T. C.

name: Worthington, Caroline Adams
description: Caroline hails from "The Delta." During the two years that she has been with us she has proved herself to be a diligent and faithful student. Caroline is an attractive girl. Although somewhat reserved, she delights in merrymaking. Her modest and dignified manner commands respect.
text: Caroline hails from "The Delta." During the two years that she has been with us she has proved herself to be a diligent and faithful student. Caroline is an attractive girl. Although somewhat reserved, she delights in merrymaking. Her modest and dignified manner commands respect.

name: Miller, Mary
motto: "Charm strikes the sight, but merit wins the soul."
text: "Charm strikes the sight, but merit wins the soul."

name: Stafford, Mildred Cato
motto: "Here's a sigh to those who love me, And a smile to those who hate; And whatever sky's above me, Here's a heart for every fate."
description: Junior Class.
text:
"Here's a sigh to those who love me, And a smile to those who hate; And whatever sky's above me, Here's a heart for every fate."
Junior Class.

name: Hill, Mildred
motto: "To be, not to be seen."
description: Such kindness, cheerfulness, and good will as Mildred possesses command the highest commendation from all. Junior Class.
text:
"To be, not to be seen."
Such kindness, cheerfulness, and good will as Mildred possesses command the highest commendation from all. Junior Class.

name: Peters, Eddie
motto: "Life is not a goblet to be drained, but a measure to be filled."
description: Eddie's pleasing personality has won for her a host of friends among students and faculty. In her classwork she is a consistent and dependable student. Junior Class.
text:
"Life is not a goblet to be drained, but a measure to be filled."
Eddie's pleasing personality has won for her a host of friends among students and faculty. In her classwork she is a consistent and dependable student. Junior Class.

name: Ingram, Eva Mae
motto:
"'Tis her privilege to lead
From joy to joy; for she can so inform
The mind that is within us; so impress
With quietness and beauty; so feed
With lofty thoughts that neither evil tongues
Nor all the dreary intercourse of daily life
Shall e'er prevail against us."
text:
"'Tis her privilege to lead
From joy to joy; for she can so inform
The mind that is within us; so impress
With quietness and beauty; so feed
With lofty thoughts that neither evil tongues
Nor all the dreary intercourse of daily life
Shall e'er prevail against us."

name: Robinson, Flynt
motto:
"Greater men than I may have lived but
I do not believe it."
text:
"Greater men than I may have lived but
I do not believe it."

name: Neel, Ola
motto:
"The elevator to success is not running;
Take the stairs."
description: We prophesy for Ola a bright and happy future. With her a thing is never merely done, but well done. Her smiles and words of cheer will make life's pathway easier to travel.

name: Tubb, J. M.
description: In the great concourse of men that have come from Monroe County, very few have been equal and none have been the superior of "Jack." Though small in stature, he has proved himself to be a man of astounding mental capacity. Jack, who is a married man with five years teaching experience, is one of the most friendly students on the campus.
text: In the great concourse of men that have come from Monroe County, very few have been equal and none have been the superior of "Jack." Though small in stature, he has proved himself to be a man of astounding mental capacity. Jack, who is a married man with five years teaching experience, is one of the most friendly students on the campus.

name: Turrittin, Gladys Etoile
description: Gladys' sunny disposition, intellectual ability, and lovable nature make her an ideal girl.
text: Gladys' sunny disposition, intellectual ability, and lovable nature make her an ideal girl.

name: Keith, Allie Mae
motto: "True merit has no limitations."
description: Allie Mae is a level-headed young lady who is inclined to the pursuit of mathematics and subjects of like difficulty. She is one of the elite who makes A's under Mr. Scott.
text:
"True merit has no limitations."
Allie Mae is a level-headed young lady who is inclined to the pursuit of mathematics and subjects of like difficulty. She is one of the elite who makes A's under Mr. Scott.

name: Thomas, Lulu James
description: A girl who is always gentle and gracious, who can make a success of her college life, who can win friends, who is competent, who has a serious mind—who could this be but Lulu?
text: A girl who is always gentle and gracious, who can make a success of her college life, who can win friends, who is competent, who has a serious mind—who could this be but Lulu?

name: Caldwell, Mary
motto: "Some may have more fun than she, But more content they cannot be; She has friends almost everywhere, Who think of her with loving care."
text: "Some may have more fun than she, But more content they cannot be; She has friends almost everywhere, Who think of her with loving care."

name: Bond, Mrs. H. H.
motto: "Whatever is worth doing is worth doing well."
description: Mrs. Bond possesses all those qualities which make a loyal friend, an earnest student, and a genial companion. She has a smile and a friendly word for everyone. She is enthusiastic whether engaged in work or play.
text:
"Whatever is worth doing is worth doing well."
Mrs. Bond possesses all those qualities which make a loyal friend, an earnest student, and a genial companion. She has a smile and a friendly word for everyone. She is enthusiastic whether engaged in work or play.

name: Kervin, Helen
motto: "Of the Past—Mindful; Of the Present—Heedful; Of the Future—Hopeful."
text: "Of the Past—Mindful; Of the Present—Heedful; Of the Future—Hopeful."

name: Newell, Grace
motto: "Game, gay, Romantic, refined, Artistic, athletic, Comforting, compassionate, Earnest, enthusiastic."
text: "Game, gay, Romantic, refined, Artistic, athletic, Comforting, compassionate, Earnest, enthusiastic."

name: Gardner, Gladys M.
motto: "Rare compound of quality, noble and true, With plenty of sense and good humor, too."
text: "Rare compound of quality, noble and true, With plenty of sense and good humor, too."
